description: DAG demo task outline — parts 1–6 (base through browser comparison + replay viewer).

submission_outline.title: Base queries (session)
submission_outline.summary: Pass hello, A, I, J, K verbatim within the wall-clock bound on each card.

submission_outline.title: Parallel fan-out
submission_outline.summary: Custom query with ≥3 independent sub-tasks as concurrent planner nodes; verify wall-clock ≈ max(branch), not sum.

submission_outline.title: Critic verdict
submission_outline.summary: Tool-grounded pass and fail (validate_json_keys); fail must splice recovery planner with corrected answer.

submission_outline.title: Coder + SandboxExecutor
submission_outline.summary: Coder prompt emits Python for SandboxExecutor; computation the formatter cannot do from text alone.

submission_outline.title: New skill (prosody analyst)
submission_outline.summary: Add one skill yaml + prompt; one query exercises it. Executor unchanged — reportable if it were.

submission_outline.title: Browser comparison agent + replay viewer
submission_outline.summary: Browser skill with four-layer cascade; comparison table + replay report (path, actions, cost). At least three visible browser actions on COMP.

design_queries.title: Parallel fan-out (≥3 independent sub-tasks)
requirements: Planner emits at least three researcher nodes with no edges between them (one concurrent wave).
Executor runs the wave with asyncio.gather — wall-clock for that layer should be ≈ max(branch elapsed), not the sum.
design_queries.verify: After run: uv run python scripts/dag/analyze_session_timing.py <session_id> — expect parallel_confirmed=true.
design_queries.also_see: Base query I (London / Paris / Berlin) uses the same parallel shape.

design_queries.title: Critic verdict (tool-grounded pass + fail with recovery)
requirements: Property verified by critic tool validate_json_keys (keys author, title, year) — available in super_browser/mcp_server.py.
Run C_pass once: critic passes → formatter only.
Run C_fail once: critic fails → recovery planner node splices in graph → corrected answer with year added.
design_queries.verify: Inspect session graph (Graph tab): C_fail session must show a recovery planner node after critic failure.

design_queries.title: New skill — yaml + prompt only (no Executor change)
requirements: Add prosody_analyst to agent_config.yaml with tools: [count_syllables] and prompt: prompts/prosody_analyst.md.
Planner routes multi-line syllable comparison through prosody_analyst → formatter (one count_syllables call per line).
Executor and flow.py need no skill-specific branch — generic SkillRegistry dispatch only.
design_queries.verify: Run PROS: graph shows planner → prosody_analyst → formatter; answer names Line B (17 syllables) over A=11, C=13.
design_queries.also_see: Tool already exists for critic; prosody_analyst is a new catalogue entry with its own prompt and routing.

design_queries.title: Browser comparison agent + replay viewer
requirements: Choose a comparison task requiring interactive browser steps (filter, sort, open detail pages).
At least three visible browser actions — not passive search snippets.
Deliver structured comparison table plus replay report: goal, DAG, path, actions, extracted data, cost.
Orchestrator unchanged — browser skill + catalogue only.
design_queries.verify: Run COMP; Graph tab → click browser node → Replay panel shows path, actions, cost. scripts/browser/analyze_browser_session.py <session_id>

browser_findings.canvas_heavy_a11y_wins: Six real canvas-heavy targets predicted to need vision all resolved on a11y via the natural cascade.
browser_findings.vision_only_on_adversarial_fixture: Vision path required only for B4 (canvas-only.html), built to defeat a11y.
browser_findings.architecture: Cascade escalates; replay trace shows path; cost ledger measures tokens/USD; skill catalogue wires into DAG runtime.

queries.label: hello
queries.title: Minimum DAG
queries.notes: Planner emits Formatter as sole successor; wall-clock under 3s.

queries.label: A
queries.title: Shannon Wikipedia
queries.query: Fetch https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Claude_Shannon and tell me his birth date, death date, and three key contributions to information theory.
queries.ui_hint: One fetch_url to Wikipedia; distiller extracts birth, death, three contributions; critic pass → formatter.
queries.verify_hint: Graph: planner → researcher (1× fetch_url) → distiller → critic (pass) → formatter.
queries.notes: Researcher fetch_url once; distiller extracts fields; critic auto-spliced; formatter terminal.

queries.label: I
queries.title: Populations parallel fan-out (canonical)
queries.query: Find the populations of London, Paris, Berlin and tell me which two are closest in size.
queries.ui_hint: Canonical parallel shape: 3 researchers in one wave, then coder and formatter.
queries.verify_hint: Same timing check as query P — analyze_session_timing.py on session id.
queries.notes: Three parallel researchers; coder; formatter diamond with auto sandbox_executor.

queries.label: J
queries.title: Graceful failure
queries.query: Read /nonexistent/path.txt and tell me what's in it.
queries.ui_hint: Missing file path — planner should route to formatter with a failure note (no hung tools).
queries.notes: Planner emits formatter directly with failure note; no tool dispatch.

queries.label: K
queries.title: Resumable execution
queries.query: For Lagos, Cairo, and Kinshasa, find current populations and growth rates and tell me which is growing fastest.
queries.ui_hint: Run once, SIGKILL mid parallel researchers, then Resume session in Graph tab (or card button below).
queries.verify_hint: Resume: running→pending; pending researcher re-runs tools; coder→formatter finish. state/sessions/<sid>/ is the durable artefact.
queries.notes: Kill mid parallel-researcher layer; resume resets running→pending.

queries.label: P
queries.title: Custom parallel fan-out (three capitals)
queries.query: Find the current population of Tokyo, Mumbai, and São Paulo and tell me which city has the largest population.
queries.ui_hint: Parallel track: three independent population lookups in one concurrent researcher wave.
queries.verify_hint: analyze_session_timing.py → parallel_confirmed=true; layer wall ≈ max(branch), not sum.

queries.label: C_pass
queries.title: Critic pass — JSON keys verified by tool
queries.query: Validate this JSON object exactly as given (do not omit fields): {"author":"Ada Lovelace","title":"Notes","year":1843}. The distiller must output this JSON verbatim. The critic must verify keys author, title, year exist using validate_json_keys.
queries.ui_hint: Run first for critic pass: validate_json_keys sees author, title, year.
queries.verify_hint: Graph: distiller → critic (pass) → formatter; no recovery planner.

queries.label: C_fail
queries.title: Critic fail — missing key triggers recovery
queries.query: Validate this JSON object exactly as given (do not add fields): {"author":"Ada Lovelace","title":"Notes"}. The distiller must output this JSON verbatim. The critic must verify keys author, title, year exist using validate_json_keys. On fail, replan with year added.
queries.ui_hint: Run second for critic fail: missing year → recovery planner splices corrected JSON.
queries.verify_hint: Graph: critic fail → recovery planner node → formatter with year:1843.

queries.label: M
queries.title: Coder + SandboxExecutor computation
queries.query: What is the exact integer value of (17 * 23 - 4) ** 2 + 1000? Use coder to compute; sandbox must verify.
queries.ui_hint: Coder emits Python; sandbox_executor verifies stdout. Expected answer: 150769.

queries.label: PROS
queries.title: Prosody analyst skill
queries.query:
Using the prosody_analyst skill, count syllables for each line below (one count_syllables call per line). Report the total for each line and which line has the most syllables:
Line A: The orchestrator runs parallel waves
Line B: Each researcher fetches population data independently
Line C: Critic validates JSON keys before formatting
queries.ui_hint: New skill: agent_config.yaml + prompts/prosody_analyst.md only. Three count_syllables calls, then compare totals.
queries.verify_hint: Line B wins: A=11, B=17, C=13 syllables (count_syllables heuristic). No Executor changes.

queries.label: COMP
queries.title: Browser comparison — Hugging Face top 3
queries.query: Compare the top 3 Hugging Face text-generation models: go to https://huggingface.co/models, filter by text-generation, filter by transformers, sort by most likes, open the top three model cards, and return a structured comparison table (model name, likes, one-line description). Use the browser skill with at least three visible actions (filter, sort, open cards). Do not use search snippets only.
queries.ui_hint: Interactive comparison + replay viewer on Graph tab (click browser node).
queries.verify_hint: Browser path likely a11y on HF; replay shows actions + cost; formatter emits comparison table.

queries.label: B1
queries.title: Layer 1 — Hacker News extract
queries.query: Using the browser skill, fetch https://news.ycombinator.com and summarize the top story listings.
queries.ui_hint: Layer 1 reference — extract path, 0 LLM turns.

queries.label: B2
queries.title: Layer 2a — Amazon deterministic
queries.query: Using browser, search Amazon for a laptop and extract title, price, brand, and description from the top product page.
queries.ui_hint: Layer 2a reference — CSS selectors, 0 LLM turns.

queries.label: B3
queries.title: Layer 2b — Hugging Face filters
queries.query: Go to https://huggingface.co/models, filter by text-generation, filter by transformers, sort by most likes, and read the top three model cards.
queries.ui_hint: Layer 2b canonical — popover dropdown turns; same site as COMP.

queries.label: B4
queries.title: Layer 3 — canvas-only coordinate vision
queries.query: Open sandbox/browser/canvas-only.html via browser. Click inside the red circle. Do not guess.
queries.ui_hint: Layer 3 reference — vision after a11y exhausts empty tree.
